scenarios.name: lock_signature_byte_flip
scenarios.description: Flip a byte in the lock receipt Ed25519 signature.

scenarios.name: exec_signature_byte_flip
scenarios.description: Flip a byte in the execution receipt Ed25519 signature.

scenarios.name: entry_weight_tamper
scenarios.description: Change the weight of the first entry. The recomputed entry hash no longer matches what was committed in the lock receipt.

scenarios.name: entry_add_fake
scenarios.description: Add a fake entry to the bundle. The recomputed entry hash differs from the committed value.

scenarios.name: drand_bls_signature_tamper
scenarios.description: Flip a byte in the bundle-level drand_signature. Does not break any receipt signature. All upstream steps pass; only BLS catches it.

scenarios.name: chain_break_lock_receipt_hash
scenarios.description: Modify the lock_receipt_hash inside the exec receipt payload and re-sign, breaking the chain linkage between lock and exec receipts while keeping the exec signature valid.

scenarios.name: seed_tamper_wrong_randomness
scenarios.description: Modify the drand_randomness inside the exec receipt payload and re-sign. The signature passes but the recomputed seed will not match.

scenarios.name: winner_rewrite
scenarios.description: Reverse the results array inside the exec receipt payload and re-sign. Seed recomputation passes (drand_randomness unchanged) but the recomputed winners will not match the reversed order.

scenarios.name: exec_entry_hash_tamper
scenarios.description: Modify the entry_hash inside the exec receipt payload (but not the lock receipt) and re-sign. Lock receipt entry hash passes; exec receipt entry hash fails.

scenarios.name: splice_exec_draw_id_mismatch
scenarios.description: Infra-level attacker signs an exec receipt declaring a different draw_id from the lock receipt, linking a valid lock_receipt_hash. Exec signature is valid under infra key; every existing step passes; the cross-receipt field consistency check catches the mismatch.

scenarios.name: splice_exec_sequence_mismatch
scenarios.description: Exec receipt declares a different sequence number from the lock receipt. Observer trusting exec.sequence for operator-log indexing is fooled without this check.

scenarios.name: splice_exec_operator_id_mismatch
scenarios.description: Exec receipt declares a different operator_id from the lock receipt. Operator-activity indexers attributing receipts are fooled without this check.

scenarios.name: splice_exec_drand_chain_mismatch
scenarios.description: Exec receipt declares a different drand chain hash from the lock receipt. Observer trusting exec.drand_chain for provenance is fooled without this check.

scenarios.name: splice_exec_drand_round_mismatch
scenarios.description: Exec receipt declares a different drand round number from the lock receipt. Observer trusting exec.drand_round for entropy timing is fooled without this check.

scenarios.name: splice_exec_weather_station_mismatch
scenarios.description: Exec receipt declares a different weather station from the lock receipt. Observer trusting exec.weather_station for entropy source identity is fooled without this check.

scenarios.name: weather_window_violation_future
scenarios.description: Exec receipt declares a weather_observation_time after the lock receipt's weather_time. Per spec §4.2.5, the observation MUST be at or before the declared target (Met Office publishes observations at hour boundaries and the worker fetches the most recent one before the target); any observation after lock.weather_time fails the window check.

scenarios.name: weather_window_violation_too_old
scenarios.description: Exec receipt declares a weather_observation_time more than 1 hour before the lock receipt's weather_time. Without the window check, an infra-level attacker could fetch weather from any point in the past and attribute it to the draw's declared window.

scenarios.name: lock_v5_with_inline_pubkey_rejects
scenarios.description: Lock receipt schema_version bumped to '5' but the bundle wrapper still carries an inline operator_public_key_hex. v5 lock receipts MUST be paired with a wrapper that omits the inline key (resolver-driven). BundleShape rejects this as a downgrade-relabel attempt.

scenarios.name: lock_v4_without_inline_pubkey_rejects
scenarios.description: Bundle wrapper for a v4 lock receipt is missing the inline operator_public_key_hex. v4 lock receipts MUST be paired with a wrapper that supplies the key. BundleShape rejects this as an upgrade-spoof attempt — accepting it would let an attacker pretend a legacy bundle is resolver-driven.

scenarios.name: exec_v2_without_inline_pubkey_rejects
scenarios.description: Bundle wrapper for a v2 execution receipt is missing the inline infrastructure_public_key_hex. v2 receipts MUST carry inline keys. BundleShape rejects this as an upgrade-spoof attempt.

scenarios.name: unknown_lock_schema_version_rejects
scenarios.description: Lock receipt declares schema_version '99' — an unknown version. Per spec §4.2.1, verifiers MUST reject unknown schemas terminally (upgrade required, no retry). BundleShape catches this via the typed dispatcher's UnknownSchemaVersion path.
